title: Why Sweating Cools You
script_summary: Exploring how sweating acts as your body's natural cooling system through evaporation.

segments.voiceover: Ever wonder why you sweat? It's your body's secret weapon against heat.
When your body heats up, it triggers sweat glands to release moisture onto your skin.
But here's the twist: that sweat doesn't cool you directly.
It's all about evaporation. As sweat evaporates, it takes heat away from your skin.
This process is called evaporative cooling, and it keeps your body temperature just right.
Next time you sweat, remember: it's your body’s way of keeping cool. Tap to learn more about your amazing biology!
